preview.summary: Algeria and Austria meet in a Group J fixture that could decide qualification fates. Both sides blend experienced leaders with emerging talent, setting up a balanced contest between Algeria's attacking flair and Austria's organized, pressing structure.
preview.homeForm: Algeria qualified comfortably from CAF with four straight wins after one defeat and beat the Netherlands in a warm-up; they sit 28th in the FIFA rankings.
preview.awayForm: Austria reached their first World Cup since 1998, topping UEFA Group H with 19 points, 22 goals scored and just four conceded under Ralf Rangnick.
preview.homeKey: Riyad Mahrez, Algeria's captain and creative driving force.
preview.awayKey: Marcel Sabitzer, second on Austria's all-time scoring list.
storylines: Veterans Alaba, Arnautovic and Sabitzer finally reach a World Cup
Rangnick's high-pressing Austria are tipped as a dark horse
Mohamed Amoura spearheads Algeria's attack after a prolific qualifying campaign
preview.h2h: Their only meeting was at the 1982 World Cup, where Austria won 2-0.
